payload.openapi_spec_content:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Transfer Service API
  description: |
    Intra-bank THB money transfer API for retail internet banking.
    Implements the Saga-driven money movement flow with idempotency, per-tx /
    daily limits, and audit-grade observability.

    Scope of this version:
      - POST /api/v1/transfers (US-001 happy path + US-003 idempotency)
      - GET  /api/v1/transfers/{transferId} (status polling)
    Stub endpoints (planned, NOT implemented in this iteration):
      - GET  /api/v1/payees/lookup        (x-status: planned, US-010)
      - GET  /api/v1/transfers             (x-status: planned, US-001 history)

    Error model: RFC 7807 Problem Details (application/problem+json).
    Auth: OAuth2 / OIDC bearer JWT (RS256, 15-min TTL).
  version: 1.0.0
  contact:
    name: Payments Platform Team
    email: payments-platform@bank.local
  license:
    name: Proprietary
servers:
  - url: https://api.bank.local
    description: Production
  - url: https://api.staging.bank.local
    description: Staging
tags:
  - name: Transfers
    description: Intra-bank money transfer operations
  - name: Payees
    description: Saved payee management (planned)
security:
  - bearerAuth: []
paths:
  /api/v1/transfers:
    post:
      tags: [Transfers]
      summary: Create an intra-bank transfer
      description: |
        Initiates a THB money transfer between two intra-bank accounts.
        The request MUST carry an `Idempotency-Key` header (UUID v4) so that
        client retries are safe — see ADR-002 + ADR-013.

        Latency target: p95 < 1s end-to-end. The endpoint runs the full Saga
        synchronously (debit + credit) and returns the final status on success.
        On a transient downstream failure (account-service circuit open) the
        endpoint returns 503 with `Retry-After`; the client SHOULD retry with
        the same Idempotency-Key.

        Error codes (machine-readable in `code`):
          INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS, TRANSACTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED, DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED,
          ACCOUNT_FROZEN, SOURCE_ACCOUNT_FROZEN, IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_CONFLICT,
          MEMO_TOO_LONG, PAYEE_NOT_FOUND, CONCURRENT_CONFLICT, COMPENSATION_TRIGGERED.
      operationId: createTransfer
      parameters:
        - $ref: '#/components/parameters/IdempotencyKey'
        - $ref: '#/components/parameters/RequestId'
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/TransferRequest'
            example:
              sourceAccountId: "11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111"
              destinationAccountId: "22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222"
              amount: "1500.0000"
              currency: "THB"
              memo: "Rent payment May 2026"
              channel: "INTERNET_BANKING"
      responses:
        '200':
          description: |
            Transfer completed (synchronous happy path). Body always carries
            `idempotencyStatus` so client can distinguish first-write vs replay.
          headers:
            X-Request-Id: { $ref: '#/components/headers/XRequestId' }
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/TransferResponse'
              examples:
                firstWrite:
                  summary: First-write success
                  value:
                    transferId: "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890"
                    referenceNumber: "TRF-20260518-0000001A"
                    status: "COMPLETED"
                    amount: "1500.0000"
                    currency: "THB"
                    sourceAccountId: "11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111"
                    destinationAccountId: "22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222"
                    memo: "Rent payment May 2026"
                    completedAt: "2026-05-18T13:14:25.123Z"
                    idempotencyStatus: "FIRST_WRITE"
                idempotentReplay:
                  summary: Idempotent replay (US-003 AC1)
                  value:
                    transferId: "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890"
                    referenceNumber: "TRF-20260518-0000001A"
                    status: "COMPLETED"
                    amount: "1500.0000"
                    currency: "THB"
                    sourceAccountId: "11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111"
                    destinationAccountId: "22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222"
                    memo: "Rent payment May 2026"
                    completedAt: "2026-05-18T13:14:25.123Z"
                    idempotencyStatus: "IDEMPOTENT_REPLAY"
        '400':
          description: Validation failure (e.g. MEMO_TOO_LONG, malformed UUID)
          headers:
            X-Request-Id: { $ref: '#/components/headers/XRequestId' }
          content:
            application/problem+json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProblemDetail' }
              example:
                type: "https://errors.bank.local/memo-too-long"
                title: "Memo too long"
                status: 400
                detail: "Memo length 257 exceeds the 200-character maximum."
                instance: "/api/v1/transfers"
                code: "MEMO_TOO_LONG"
                traceId: "00-7e3f9a1b2c3d4e5f6789abcdef012345-0123456789abcdef-01"
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized'
        '403':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden'
        '409':
          description: |
            Idempotency-Key conflict (US-003 AC4) — same key submitted with a
            different request payload.
          headers:
            X-Request-Id: { $ref: '#/components/headers/XRequestId' }
          content:
            application/problem+json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProblemDetail' }
              example:
                type: "https://errors.bank.local/idempotency-key-conflict"
                title: "Idempotency-Key conflict"
                status: 409
                detail: "Idempotency-Key was previously used with a different request payload."
                instance: "/api/v1/transfers"
                code: "IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_CONFLICT"
                traceId: "00-7e3f9a1b...-01"
        '422':
          description: |
            Business rule violation. Inspect `code` for the specific reason:
            INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS, TRANSACTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED, DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED,
            SOURCE_ACCOUNT_FROZEN, ACCOUNT_FROZEN, PAYEE_NOT_FOUND,
            CONCURRENT_CONFLICT, COMPENSATION_TRIGGERED.
          headers:
            X-Request-Id: { $ref: '#/components/headers/XRequestId' }
          content:
            application/problem+json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProblemDetail' }
              examples:
                insufficientFunds:
                  summary: US-002 — insufficient balance
                  value:
                    type: "https://errors.bank.local/insufficient-funds"
                    title: "Insufficient funds"
                    status: 422
                    detail: "Source account available balance is below the requested amount."
                    instance: "/api/v1/transfers"
                    code: "INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS"
                    traceId: "00-...-01"
                dailyLimitExceeded:
                  summary: US-005 — daily limit exceeded
                  value:
                    type: "https://errors.bank.local/daily-limit-exceeded"
                    title: "Daily limit exceeded"
                    status: 422
                    detail: "This transfer would push your daily cumulative transfers past the configured limit."
                    instance: "/api/v1/transfers"
                    code: "DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED"
                    traceId: "00-...-01"
        '429':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/RateLimited'
        '500':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/InternalError'
        '503':
          description: |
            Downstream dependency unavailable (account-service circuit open or
            time-limiter hit). Client SHOULD retry with the same Idempotency-Key
            after `Retry-After` seconds.
          headers:
            X-Request-Id: { $ref: '#/components/headers/XRequestId' }
            Retry-After:
              schema: { type: integer, example: 2 }
              description: Seconds to wait before retrying.
          content:
            application/problem+json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProblemDetail' }
              example:
                type: "https://errors.bank.local/dependency-unavailable"
                title: "Service temporarily unavailable"
                status: 503
                detail: "Downstream account-service is unavailable. Please retry."
                instance: "/api/v1/transfers"
                code: "DEPENDENCY_UNAVAILABLE"
                traceId: "00-...-01"
  /api/v1/transfers/{transferId}:
    get:
      tags: [Transfers]
      summary: Get transfer status
      description: |
        Fetches the current status of a transfer by id. Used by the client to
        poll after a 503 or socket-level timeout to determine whether the
        original request committed.

        Authorization: the calling JWT subject MUST be the initiator of the
        transfer (or hold the ops:transfers:read scope).
      operationId: getTransfer
      parameters:
        - $ref: '#/components/parameters/TransferId'
        - $ref: '#/components/parameters/RequestId'
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Current transfer state
          headers:
            X-Request-Id: { $ref: '#/components/headers/XRequestId' }
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/TransferResponse' }
              example:
                transferId: "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890"
                referenceNumber: "TRF-20260518-0000001A"
                status: "COMPLETED"
                amount: "1500.0000"
                currency: "THB"
                sourceAccountId: "11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111"
                destinationAccountId: "22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222"
                memo: "Rent payment May 2026"
                completedAt: "2026-05-18T13:14:25.123Z"
                idempotencyStatus: "FIRST_WRITE"
        '401':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized'
        '403':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden'
        '404':
          description: Transfer not found
          headers:
            X-Request-Id: { $ref: '#/components/headers/XRequestId' }
          content:
            application/problem+json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProblemDetail' }
              example:
                type: "https://errors.bank.local/transfer-not-found"
                title: "Transfer not found"
                status: 404
                detail: "No transfer exists with id a1b2c3d4-..."
                instance: "/api/v1/transfers/a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890"
                code: "TRANSFER_NOT_FOUND"
                traceId: "00-...-01"
        '500':
          $ref: '#/components/responses/InternalError'
  /api/v1/payees/lookup:
    get:
      tags: [Payees]
      summary: Look up payee details by account number (PLANNED)
      description: |
        Returns masked name + account type for a given intra-bank account number.
        NOT implemented in this iteration — stub only. See US-010.
      operationId: lookupPayee
      x-status: planned
      x-planned-story: US-010
      parameters:
        - in: query
          name: accountNumber
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
            pattern: "^[0-9]{10}$"
            example: "1234567890"
      responses:
        '501':
          description: Not implemented in this iteration
          content:
            application/problem+json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProblemDetail' }
  /api/v1/transfers/history:
    get:
      tags: [Transfers]
      summary: List transfers for the current customer (PLANNED)
      description: |
        Paginated list of the caller's transfers. NOT implemented in this
        iteration — stub only. See US-001 history requirement.
      operationId: listTransfers
      x-status: planned
      x-planned-story: US-001-history
      parameters:
        - in: query
          name: page
          schema: { type: integer, minimum: 0, default: 0 }
        - in: query
          name: size
          schema: { type: integer, minimum: 1, maximum: 100, default: 20 }
        - in: query
          name: sort
          schema: { type: string, example: "createdAt,desc" }
      responses:
        '501':
          description: Not implemented in this iteration
          content:
            application/problem+json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProblemDetail' }
components:
  securitySchemes:
    bearerAuth:
      type: http
      scheme: bearer
      bearerFormat: JWT
      description: |
        OAuth2 / OIDC RS256 access token issued by identity-service.
        TTL 15 minutes; refresh-token rotation per ADR-002 of solution architect.
  parameters:
    IdempotencyKey:
      in: header
      name: Idempotency-Key
      required: true
      schema:
        type: string
        format: uuid
        example: "d2c3b4a5-1111-4222-9333-444455556666"
      description: |
        Client-generated UUID v4. Scope: per (customer_id, key). TTL: 24h.
        Reusing the same key with the same payload returns the cached response
        (idempotencyStatus=IDEMPOTENT_REPLAY). Reusing with a different payload
        returns 409 IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_CONFLICT. See ADR-013.
    RequestId:
      in: header
      name: X-Request-Id
      required: false
      schema:
        type: string
        maxLength: 128
        example: "client-12345-abcde"
      description: |
        Optional client-provided correlation id. If absent, the gateway / service
        assigns one. Always echoed in the response `X-Request-Id` header and
        included in the W3C `traceparent` propagation.
    TransferId:
      in: path
      name: transferId
      required: true
      schema:
        type: string
        format: uuid
      description: Server-assigned transfer identifier (UUID v4).
  headers:
    XRequestId:
      schema: { type: string }
      description: Echoed correlation id. Present on every response.
  responses:
    Unauthorized:
      description: Missing or invalid bearer token
      headers:
        X-Request-Id: { $ref: '#/components/headers/XRequestId' }
      content:
        application/problem+json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProblemDetail' }
          example:
            type: "https://errors.bank.local/unauthorized"
            title: "Unauthorized"
            status: 401
            detail: "Bearer token missing, expired, or signature invalid."
            code: "UNAUTHORIZED"
            traceId: "00-...-01"
    Forbidden:
      description: Authenticated but not authorized for this resource
      headers:
        X-Request-Id: { $ref: '#/components/headers/XRequestId' }
      content:
        application/problem+json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProblemDetail' }
          example:
            type: "https://errors.bank.local/forbidden"
            title: "Forbidden"
            status: 403
            detail: "You do not own this resource and lack the required scope."
            code: "FORBIDDEN"
            traceId: "00-...-01"
    RateLimited:
      description: Rate limit exceeded (60 req/min per customer at gateway)
      headers:
        X-Request-Id: { $ref: '#/components/headers/XRequestId' }
        Retry-After:
          schema: { type: integer }
          description: Seconds to wait before next request.
      content:
        application/problem+json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProblemDetail' }
          example:
            type: "https://errors.bank.local/rate-limited"
            title: "Too many requests"
            status: 429
            detail: "Per-customer rate limit of 60 requests/minute exceeded."
            code: "RATE_LIMITED"
            traceId: "00-...-01"
    InternalError:
      description: Unexpected server error
      headers:
        X-Request-Id: { $ref: '#/components/headers/XRequestId' }
      content:
        application/problem+json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProblemDetail' }
          example:
            type: "https://errors.bank.local/internal-error"
            title: "Internal server error"
            status: 500
            detail: "An unexpected error occurred. The request was not committed."
            code: "INTERNAL_ERROR"
            traceId: "00-...-01"
  schemas:
    TransferRequest:
      type: object
      required: [sourceAccountId, destinationAccountId, amount, currency]
      properties:
        sourceAccountId:
          type: string
          format: uuid
          description: UUID of the customer's source account.
        destinationAccountId:
          type: string
          format: uuid
          description: UUID of the intra-bank destination account.
        amount:
          type: string
          pattern: "^[0-9]+(\\.[0-9]{1,4})?$"
          minLength: 1
          maxLength: 23
          description: |
            Decimal money amount as a string (JSON number would lose precision).
            Must be > 0 and <= per-tx limit. Scale up to 4 decimal places.
          example: "1500.0000"
        currency:
          type: string
          enum: [THB]
          description: ISO 4217. v1 supports THB only.
          example: "THB"
        memo:
          type: string
          maxLength: 200
          description: Optional free-text memo, max 200 characters (US-001 AC3/AC4).
          example: "Rent payment May 2026"
        channel:
          type: string
          enum: [INTERNET_BANKING, MOBILE_BANKING]
          default: INTERNET_BANKING
          description: Origination channel (audited).
    TransferResponse:
      type: object
      required: [transferId, referenceNumber, status, amount, currency, sourceAccountId, destinationAccountId, idempotencyStatus]
      properties:
        transferId:
          type: string
          format: uuid
          description: Server-assigned transfer id.
        referenceNumber:
          type: string
          description: Human-readable reference TRF-YYYYMMDD-XXXXXXXX.
          example: "TRF-20260518-0000001A"
        status:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/TransferStatus'
        amount:
          type: string
          pattern: "^[0-9]+(\\.[0-9]{1,4})?$"
          example: "1500.0000"
        currency:
          type: string
          enum: [THB]
        sourceAccountId:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        destinationAccountId:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        memo:
          type: string
          nullable: true
          maxLength: 200
        completedAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
          nullable: true
          description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when status became terminal.
        idempotencyStatus:
          type: string
          enum: [FIRST_WRITE, IDEMPOTENT_REPLAY]
          description: |
            FIRST_WRITE — this response is from executing the request now.
            IDEMPOTENT_REPLAY — cached response served for a duplicate key.
        failureReason:
          type: string
          nullable: true
          description: Populated when status is FAILED / FAILED_COMPENSATED / COMPENSATION_FAILED.
          example: "INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS"
    TransferStatus:
      type: string
      enum:
        - PENDING
        - COMPLETED
        - FAILED
        - COMPENSATION_PENDING
        - FAILED_COMPENSATED
        - COMPENSATION_FAILED
      description: |
        State machine:
          PENDING -> COMPLETED                        (happy path)
          PENDING -> FAILED                           (rejected pre-debit)
          PENDING -> COMPENSATION_PENDING -> FAILED_COMPENSATED   (saga rollback ok)
          PENDING -> COMPENSATION_PENDING -> COMPENSATION_FAILED  (double fault)
    ProblemDetail:
      type: object
      description: RFC 7807 Problem Details extended with `code` and `traceId`.
      required: [type, title, status, code]
      properties:
        type:
          type: string
          format: uri
          description: A URI reference identifying the problem type.
          example: "https://errors.bank.local/insufficient-funds"
        title:
          type: string
          description: Short human-readable summary.
          example: "Insufficient funds"
        status:
          type: integer
          minimum: 100
          maximum: 599
          example: 422
        detail:
          type: string
          description: Human-readable explanation. SAFE for end-user display (no PII / no secrets).
          example: "Source account balance is below the requested amount."
        instance:
          type: string
          format: uri
          description: URI of the specific occurrence of the problem.
          example: "/api/v1/transfers"
        code:
          type: string
          description: Machine-readable error code from the taxonomy.
          enum:
            - INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS
            - TRANSACTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
            - DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
            - ACCOUNT_FROZEN
            - SOURCE_ACCOUNT_FROZEN
            - ACCOUNT_INACTIVE
            - SOURCE_ACCOUNT_INACTIVE
            - IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_CONFLICT
            - MEMO_TOO_LONG
            - PAYEE_NOT_FOUND
            - TRANSFER_NOT_FOUND
            - CONCURRENT_CONFLICT
            - COMPENSATION_TRIGGERED
            - DEPENDENCY_UNAVAILABLE
            - RATE_LIMITED
            - UNAUTHORIZED
            - FORBIDDEN
            - INTERNAL_ERROR
            - VALIDATION_ERROR
        traceId:
          type: string
          description: W3C traceparent id for correlation across services.
          example: "00-7e3f9a1b2c3d4e5f6789abcdef012345-0123456789abcdef-01"

migration_files.content:
-- V001__create_transfers.sql
-- Ticket: TRANSFER-17
-- Author: banking-tech-lead
-- Rationale: Core transfer aggregate for the money-transfer Saga. Holds the
--            business state machine (PENDING -> COMPLETED / FAILED /
--            FAILED_COMPENSATED / COMPENSATION_FAILED) plus references to
--            saga state and the compensating transfer (US-008).
--            Schema designed so US-001/003 backend dev can implement now, and
--            US-004..US-011 can land later without breaking changes.
-- Down (runbook only — Flyway does not auto-run):
--   DROP TABLE IF EXISTS transfers;

CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pgcrypto;

CREATE TABLE transfers (
    transfer_id              UUID            PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    reference_number         VARCHAR(32)     NOT NULL,
    source_account_id        UUID            NOT NULL,
    destination_account_id   UUID            NOT NULL,
    initiator_user_id        UUID            NOT NULL,
    initiator_customer_id    UUID            NOT NULL,
    amount                   NUMERIC(19,4)   NOT NULL CHECK (amount > 0),
    currency                 CHAR(3)         NOT NULL CHECK (currency = 'THB'),
    memo                     VARCHAR(200),
    status                   VARCHAR(32)     NOT NULL
        CHECK (status IN (
            'PENDING',
            'COMPLETED',
            'FAILED',
            'COMPENSATION_PENDING',
            'FAILED_COMPENSATED',
            'COMPENSATION_FAILED'
        )),
    channel                  VARCHAR(32)     NOT NULL
        CHECK (channel IN ('INTERNET_BANKING','MOBILE_BANKING')),
    saga_id                  UUID,
    compensation_transfer_id UUID,
    failure_reason           VARCHAR(64),
    limit_snapshot_per_tx    NUMERIC(19,4),
    limit_snapshot_daily     NUMERIC(19,4),
    correlation_id           VARCHAR(64)     NOT NULL,
    version                  BIGINT          NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at               TIMESTAMPTZ     NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at               TIMESTAMPTZ     NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    completed_at             TIMESTAMPTZ,
    CONSTRAINT uq_transfers_reference_number UNIQUE (reference_number),
    CONSTRAINT fk_transfers_compensation
        FOREIGN KEY (compensation_transfer_id) REFERENCES transfers(transfer_id)
);

-- Read pattern: customer transfer history page (US-001 history endpoint, planned).
COMMENT ON TABLE transfers IS 'Money transfer aggregate root (intra-bank, THB). State machine per ADR-001.';
CREATE INDEX idx_transfers_initiator_customer_created_at
    ON transfers (initiator_customer_id, created_at DESC);
COMMENT ON INDEX idx_transfers_initiator_customer_created_at IS
    'Read pattern: list current customer transfers most-recent-first (US-001 history).';

-- Read pattern: list transfers per source account (ops investigations, US-006/007).
CREATE INDEX idx_transfers_source_account_created_at
    ON transfers (source_account_id, created_at DESC);
COMMENT ON INDEX idx_transfers_source_account_created_at IS
    'Read pattern: per-account transfer history for ops + reconciliation.';

-- Read pattern: saga recovery on pod restart (US-008 AC4, ADR-001).
CREATE INDEX idx_transfers_status_updated_at
    ON transfers (status, updated_at)
    WHERE status IN ('PENDING','COMPENSATION_PENDING','COMPENSATION_FAILED');
COMMENT ON INDEX idx_transfers_status_updated_at IS
    'Partial index for saga recovery scan: in-flight + failed-compensation states only.';

-- V002__create_transfer_idempotency.sql
-- Ticket: TRANSFER-18
-- Author: banking-tech-lead
-- Rationale: Per-service idempotency table per ADR-002 of solution-arch.
--            Holds SHA-256 hash of the Idempotency-Key (scoped per customer),
--            request payload checksum for conflict detection (US-003 AC4),
--            and the cached response body for replay (US-003 AC1/AC2).
--            Atomic with the business write — same DB transaction as transfers.
-- Down (runbook only):
--   DROP TABLE IF EXISTS transfer_idempotency;

CREATE TABLE transfer_idempotency (
    key_hash             CHAR(64)        NOT NULL,
    owner_customer_id    UUID            NOT NULL,
    transfer_id          UUID,
    request_checksum     CHAR(64)        NOT NULL,
    result_status        VARCHAR(32)     NOT NULL
        CHECK (result_status IN (
            'PENDING','COMPLETED','FAILED','REJECTED','CONFLICT'
        )),
    cached_response_code SMALLINT        NOT NULL,
    cached_response_body JSONB           NOT NULL,
    correlation_id       VARCHAR(64)     NOT NULL,
    created_at           TIMESTAMPTZ     NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    expires_at           TIMESTAMPTZ     NOT NULL,
    CONSTRAINT pk_transfer_idempotency PRIMARY KEY (key_hash, owner_customer_id),
    CONSTRAINT fk_transfer_idempotency_transfer
        FOREIGN KEY (transfer_id) REFERENCES transfers(transfer_id),
    CONSTRAINT chk_transfer_idempotency_ttl
        CHECK (expires_at > created_at)
);

COMMENT ON TABLE transfer_idempotency IS
    'Idempotency-Key store, SHA-256 hashed, scoped per (key, customer). TTL 24h. See ADR-013.';

-- Read pattern: hourly TTL purge job (ADR-013 / RISK-004).
CREATE INDEX idx_transfer_idempotency_expires_at
    ON transfer_idempotency (expires_at);
COMMENT ON INDEX idx_transfer_idempotency_expires_at IS
    'Read pattern: TTL cleanup job DELETE WHERE expires_at < now() LIMIT N.';

-- V003__create_saga_state.sql
-- Ticket: TRANSFER-19
-- Author: banking-tech-lead
-- Rationale: Persistent saga state machine per ADR-001 (orchestration).
--            Survives pod restarts so a recovery worker can resume in-flight
--            sagas (US-008 AC4 + RISK-001). One row per saga; transitions are
--            recorded by updating current_step + last_event + version.
-- Down (runbook only):
--   DROP TABLE IF EXISTS saga_state;

CREATE TABLE saga_state (
    saga_id              UUID            PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    transfer_id          UUID            NOT NULL,
    saga_type            VARCHAR(64)     NOT NULL
        CHECK (saga_type IN ('INTRA_BANK_TRANSFER')),
    status               VARCHAR(32)     NOT NULL
        CHECK (status IN (
            'IN_PROGRESS',
            'COMPLETED',
            'COMPENSATING',
            'COMPENSATED',
            'COMPENSATION_FAILED'
        )),
    current_step         VARCHAR(64)     NOT NULL
        CHECK (current_step IN (
            'STARTED',
            'LIMIT_CHECK_DONE',
            'DEBITED',
            'CREDITED',
            'COMPLETED',
            'COMPENSATION_DEBIT_REVERSED',
            'COMPENSATION_FAILED'
        )),
    last_event           VARCHAR(64),
    retry_count          INTEGER         NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK (retry_count >= 0),
    last_error           TEXT,
    context              JSONB           NOT NULL,
    correlation_id       VARCHAR(64)     NOT NULL,
    version              BIGINT          NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at           TIMESTAMPTZ     NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at           TIMESTAMPTZ     NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    completed_at         TIMESTAMPTZ,
    CONSTRAINT uq_saga_state_transfer UNIQUE (transfer_id),
    CONSTRAINT fk_saga_state_transfer
        FOREIGN KEY (transfer_id) REFERENCES transfers(transfer_id)
);

COMMENT ON TABLE saga_state IS
    'Persistent saga coordinator state. One row per saga. Survives pod restarts (ADR-001, US-008).';

-- Read pattern: recovery worker picks up unfinished sagas after restart.
CREATE INDEX idx_saga_state_status_updated_at
    ON saga_state (status, updated_at)
    WHERE status IN ('IN_PROGRESS','COMPENSATING','COMPENSATION_FAILED');
COMMENT ON INDEX idx_saga_state_status_updated_at IS
    'Read pattern: saga recovery scan for unfinished sagas (RISK-001 mitigation).';

-- V004__create_outbox.sql
-- Ticket: TRANSFER-20
-- Author: banking-tech-lead
-- Rationale: Transactional outbox per architect ADR-003.
--            Business write + outbox row committed in the same DB transaction;
--            background relay polls and publishes to Kafka with SKIP LOCKED.
--            Marks dispatched=true on Kafka ack. See ADR-014 for poll cadence.
-- Down (runbook only):
--   DROP TABLE IF EXISTS transfer_outbox;

CREATE TABLE transfer_outbox (
    outbox_id          UUID            PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    aggregate_type     VARCHAR(64)     NOT NULL
        CHECK (aggregate_type IN ('TRANSFER','AML_CASE')),
    aggregate_id       UUID            NOT NULL,
    event_type         VARCHAR(64)     NOT NULL
        CHECK (event_type IN (
            'TransferRequested',
            'TransferCompleted',
            'TransferFailed',
            'TransferCompensated',
            'AmlThresholdBreached'
        )),
    event_id           UUID            NOT NULL,
    schema_version     SMALLINT        NOT NULL DEFAULT 1 CHECK (schema_version > 0),
    topic              VARCHAR(128)    NOT NULL,
    partition_key      VARCHAR(128)    NOT NULL,
    headers            JSONB           NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
    payload            JSONB           NOT NULL,
    correlation_id     VARCHAR(64)     NOT NULL,
    dispatched         BOOLEAN         NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
    dispatched_at      TIMESTAMPTZ,
    attempt_count      INTEGER         NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK (attempt_count >= 0),
    last_error         TEXT,
    created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ     NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    CONSTRAINT uq_transfer_outbox_event_id UNIQUE (event_id)
);

COMMENT ON TABLE transfer_outbox IS
    'Transactional outbox for Kafka publishing. Polled by in-service relay (ADR-014).';

-- Read pattern: relay worker — SELECT ... WHERE dispatched=false FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED.
CREATE INDEX idx_transfer_outbox_undispatched
    ON transfer_outbox (created_at)
    WHERE dispatched = false;
COMMENT ON INDEX idx_transfer_outbox_undispatched IS
    'Read pattern: relay polls oldest-first undispatched rows with SKIP LOCKED.';

-- V005__create_daily_transfer_accumulator.sql
-- Ticket: TRANSFER-21
-- Author: banking-tech-lead
-- Rationale: Daily cumulative limit (US-005) + AML threshold (US-009) state.
--            Per architect ADR-004: PostgreSQL row with optimistic lock
--            updated in the same transaction as the transfer write.
--            Per ADR-006: accumulation_date stores Bangkok (UTC+7) civil date.
--            Per ADR-007: keyed by account_id; customer-level aggregation
--            handled via a view if Compliance later requires it.
--            US-001/003 backend dev does NOT touch this table yet, but schema
--            is here so subsequent stories can land without migration churn.
-- Down (runbook only):
--   DROP TABLE IF EXISTS daily_transfer_accumulator;

CREATE TABLE daily_transfer_accumulator (
    accumulator_id          UUID            PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    account_id              UUID            NOT NULL,
    customer_id             UUID            NOT NULL,
    accumulation_date       DATE            NOT NULL,
    cumulative_amount       NUMERIC(19,4)   NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK (cumulative_amount >= 0),
    transfer_count          INTEGER         NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK (transfer_count >= 0),
    aml_threshold_breached  BOOLEAN         NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
    aml_breach_notified_at  TIMESTAMPTZ,
    version                 BIGINT          NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at              TIMESTAMPTZ     NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at              TIMESTAMPTZ     NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    CONSTRAINT uq_accumulator_account_date UNIQUE (account_id, accumulation_date)
);

COMMENT ON TABLE daily_transfer_accumulator IS
    'Per-account per-Bangkok-civil-day cumulative transfer counters. Used for daily limit (US-005) + AML threshold (US-009). Optimistic locked.';
COMMENT ON COLUMN daily_transfer_accumulator.accumulation_date IS
    'Bangkok (UTC+7) civil date — see ADR-006.';

-- Read pattern: customer-level rollup for AML scope (ADR-007 alternative path).
CREATE INDEX idx_accumulator_customer_date
    ON daily_transfer_accumulator (customer_id, accumulation_date);
COMMENT ON INDEX idx_accumulator_customer_date IS
    'Read pattern: future customer-level AML aggregation (ADR-007 hedge).';

db_schema.erd_mermaid:
erDiagram
    transfers ||--o| saga_state : "has 0..1"
    transfers ||--o{ transfer_idempotency : "may be cached by"
    transfers ||--o{ transfer_outbox : "emits events into"
    transfers ||--o| transfers : "compensation_transfer_id"
    daily_transfer_accumulator ||--o{ transfers : "per-account-per-Bangkok-day rollup of"

    transfers {
        UUID transfer_id PK
        VARCHAR reference_number UK
        UUID source_account_id
        UUID destination_account_id
        UUID initiator_user_id
        UUID initiator_customer_id
        NUMERIC amount
        CHAR currency
        VARCHAR memo
        VARCHAR status
        VARCHAR channel
        UUID saga_id
        UUID compensation_transfer_id FK
        VARCHAR failure_reason
        NUMERIC limit_snapshot_per_tx
        NUMERIC limit_snapshot_daily
        VARCHAR correlation_id
        BIGINT version
        TIMESTAMPTZ created_at
        TIMESTAMPTZ updated_at
        TIMESTAMPTZ completed_at
    }
    transfer_idempotency {
        CHAR key_hash PK
        UUID owner_customer_id PK
        UUID transfer_id FK
        CHAR request_checksum
        VARCHAR result_status
        SMALLINT cached_response_code
        JSONB cached_response_body
        VARCHAR correlation_id
        TIMESTAMPTZ created_at
        TIMESTAMPTZ expires_at
    }
    saga_state {
        UUID saga_id PK
        UUID transfer_id FK
        VARCHAR saga_type
        VARCHAR status
        VARCHAR current_step
        VARCHAR last_event
        INTEGER retry_count
        TEXT last_error
        JSONB context
        VARCHAR correlation_id
        BIGINT version
        TIMESTAMPTZ created_at
        TIMESTAMPTZ updated_at
        TIMESTAMPTZ completed_at
    }
    transfer_outbox {
        UUID outbox_id PK
        VARCHAR aggregate_type
        UUID aggregate_id
        VARCHAR event_type
        UUID event_id UK
        SMALLINT schema_version
        VARCHAR topic
        VARCHAR partition_key
        JSONB headers
        JSONB payload
        VARCHAR correlation_id
        BOOLEAN dispatched
        TIMESTAMPTZ dispatched_at
        INTEGER attempt_count
        TEXT last_error
        TIMESTAMPTZ created_at
    }
    daily_transfer_accumulator {
        UUID accumulator_id PK
        UUID account_id
        UUID customer_id
        DATE accumulation_date
        NUMERIC cumulative_amount
        INTEGER transfer_count
        BOOLEAN aml_threshold_breached
        TIMESTAMPTZ aml_breach_notified_at
        BIGINT version
        TIMESTAMPTZ created_at
        TIMESTAMPTZ updated_at
    }

event_schemas.name: TransferRequested.avsc
event_schemas.content:
{
  "type": "record",
  "name": "TransferRequested",
  "namespace": "com.bank.payments.transfer.v1",
  "doc": "Emitted when a transfer request has been accepted, validated, and persisted as PENDING in transfer-service. Published via transactional outbox. Topic: transfer.transfer.requested.v1",
  "fields": [
    { "name": "eventId",              "type": { "type": "string", "logicalType": "uuid" }, "doc": "UUID v4 — used by consumers to dedupe (outbox is at-least-once)." },
    { "name": "eventType",            "type": "string", "default": "TransferRequested" },
    { "name": "schemaVersion",        "type": "int",    "default": 1 },
    { "name": "occurredAt",           "type": { "type": "long", "logicalType": "timestamp-millis" }, "doc": "UTC." },
    { "name": "correlationId",        "type": "string" },
    { "name": "transferId",           "type": { "type": "string", "logicalType": "uuid" } },
    { "name": "referenceNumber",      "type": "string" },
    { "name": "sourceAccountId",      "type": { "type": "string", "logicalType": "uuid" } },
    { "name": "destinationAccountId", "type": { "type": "string", "logicalType": "uuid" } },
    { "name": "initiatorUserId",      "type": { "type": "string", "logicalType": "uuid" } },
    { "name": "initiatorCustomerId",  "type": { "type": "string", "logicalType": "uuid" } },
    { "name": "amount",               "type": { "type": "bytes", "logicalType": "decimal", "precision": 19, "scale": 4 } },
    { "name": "currency",             "type": "string", "default": "THB" },
    { "name": "channel",              "type": { "type": "enum", "name": "Channel", "symbols": ["INTERNET_BANKING","MOBILE_BANKING"] } },
    { "name": "idempotencyKeyHash",   "type": "string", "doc": "SHA-256 hex — not the raw key." }
  ]
}

event_schemas.name: TransferCompleted.avsc
event_schemas.content:
{
  "type": "record",
  "name": "TransferCompleted",
  "namespace": "com.bank.payments.transfer.v1",
  "doc": "Emitted after both debit + credit have committed and transfer.status = COMPLETED. Topic: transfer.transfer.completed.v1",
  "fields": [
    { "name": "eventId",              "type": { "type": "string", "logicalType": "uuid" } },
    { "name": "eventType",            "type": "string", "default": "TransferCompleted" },
    { "name": "schemaVersion",        "type": "int",    "default": 1 },
    { "name": "occurredAt",           "type": { "type": "long", "logicalType": "timestamp-millis" } },
    { "name": "correlationId",        "type": "string" },
    { "name": "transferId",           "type": { "type": "string", "logicalType": "uuid" } },
    { "name": "referenceNumber",      "type": "string" },
    { "name": "sourceAccountId",      "type": { "type": "string", "logicalType": "uuid" } },
    { "name": "destinationAccountId", "type": { "type": "string", "logicalType": "uuid" } },
    { "name": "initiatorCustomerId",  "type": { "type": "string", "logicalType": "uuid" } },
    { "name": "amount",               "type": { "type": "bytes", "logicalType": "decimal", "precision": 19, "scale": 4 } },
    { "name": "currency",             "type": "string", "default": "THB" },
    { "name": "debitJournalEntryId",  "type": ["null", { "type": "string", "logicalType": "uuid" }], "default": null },
    { "name": "creditJournalEntryId", "type": ["null", { "type": "string", "logicalType": "uuid" }], "default": null },
    { "name": "completedAt",          "type": { "type": "long", "logicalType": "timestamp-millis" } }
  ]
}

event_schemas.name: TransferFailed.avsc
event_schemas.content:
{
  "type": "record",
  "name": "TransferFailed",
  "namespace": "com.bank.payments.transfer.v1",
  "doc": "Emitted when a transfer transitions to FAILED, FAILED_COMPENSATED, or COMPENSATION_FAILED. Topic: transfer.transfer.failed.v1",
  "fields": [
    { "name": "eventId",              "type": { "type": "string", "logicalType": "uuid" } },
    { "name": "eventType",            "type": "string", "default": "TransferFailed" },
    { "name": "schemaVersion",        "type": "int",    "default": 1 },
    { "name": "occurredAt",           "type": { "type": "long", "logicalType": "timestamp-millis" } },
    { "name": "correlationId",        "type": "string" },
    { "name": "transferId",           "type": { "type": "string", "logicalType": "uuid" } },
    { "name": "referenceNumber",      "type": "string" },
    { "name": "sourceAccountId",      "type": { "type": "string", "logicalType": "uuid" } },
    { "name": "destinationAccountId", "type": { "type": "string", "logicalType": "uuid" } },
    { "name": "initiatorCustomerId",  "type": { "type": "string", "logicalType": "uuid" } },
    { "name": "amount",               "type": { "type": "bytes", "logicalType": "decimal", "precision": 19, "scale": 4 } },
    { "name": "currency",             "type": "string", "default": "THB" },
    { "name": "failureReason",
      "type": { "type": "enum", "name": "FailureReason",
                "symbols": [
                  "INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS",
                  "TRANSACTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED",
                  "DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED",
                  "SOURCE_ACCOUNT_FROZEN",
                  "SOURCE_ACCOUNT_INACTIVE",
                  "ACCOUNT_FROZEN",
                  "ACCOUNT_INACTIVE",
                  "PAYEE_NOT_FOUND",
                  "CONCURRENT_CONFLICT",
                  "CREDIT_STEP_FAILED",
                  "COMPENSATION_FAILED",
                  "VALIDATION_ERROR",
                  "DEPENDENCY_UNAVAILABLE"
                ] } },
    { "name": "failureStage",
      "type": { "type": "enum", "name": "FailureStage",
                "symbols": [
                  "VALIDATION",
                  "PRECHECK",
                  "DEBIT",
                  "CREDIT",
                  "COMPENSATION"
                ] } },
    { "name": "compensationTransferId", "type": ["null", { "type": "string", "logicalType": "uuid" }], "default": null },
    { "name": "failedAt",              "type": { "type": "long", "logicalType": "timestamp-millis" } }
  ]
}

adrs.title: Error code taxonomy + Idempotency-Key hashing strategy
adrs.content:
# ADR-013. Error code taxonomy + Idempotency-Key hashing strategy

- Status: accepted
- Date: 2026-05-18
- Deciders: banking-tech-lead
- Tags: api-contract, idempotency, security

## Context and Problem
The Architect's ADR-002 fixed the storage shape for idempotency (per-service PostgreSQL table, 24h TTL). The Tech Lead must now pin:
1. The exact taxonomy of error codes the API surfaces (machine-readable `code` in Problem-Detail), so backend, frontend, QA, and ops all agree.
2. The hashing + scoping rule for the Idempotency-Key (raw key vs hash, per-customer scope vs global, payload checksum algorithm).
This is the kind of decision that, if left implicit, becomes painful at iteration 4 (frontend ships a different code, ops dashboard rules drift).

## Decision Drivers
- Backend, frontend, QA, ops must use the same vocabulary.
- No raw secrets-like strings on disk (operator could brute force Idempotency-Keys back to requests).
- Replays must be exactly the previous response, byte-for-byte.
- Payload mismatch on the same key must be deterministically detectable.

## Considered Options
1. Raw key stored as PK; global uniqueness across customers.
2. SHA-256(key) PK; per-customer scope (composite PK: `(key_hash, customer_id)`); payload SHA-256 checksum column.
3. HMAC-SHA-256(key, server-secret); same scope as option 2.

## Decision Outcome
Chosen: Option 2 — `key_hash = SHA-256(raw_key)`, composite PK `(key_hash, owner_customer_id)`, `request_checksum = SHA-256(canonical_json(request_body))`.

Error code taxonomy frozen at the values enumerated in the OpenAPI `ProblemDetail.code` enum: `INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS, TRANSACTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED, DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED, ACCOUNT_FROZEN, SOURCE_ACCOUNT_FROZEN, ACCOUNT_INACTIVE, SOURCE_ACCOUNT_INACTIVE, IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_CONFLICT, MEMO_TOO_LONG, PAYEE_NOT_FOUND, TRANSFER_NOT_FOUND, CONCURRENT_CONFLICT, COMPENSATION_TRIGGERED, DEPENDENCY_UNAVAILABLE, RATE_LIMITED, UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, INTERNAL_ERROR, VALIDATION_ERROR`. Adding a new code requires a new ADR.

Idempotency algorithm (deterministic):
1. Receive request → extract `Idempotency-Key` header (validate UUID v4 shape; reject 400 if missing/malformed).
2. `key_hash = SHA-256_hex(raw_key)`.
3. `request_checksum = SHA-256_hex(canonical_json(body))` where canonical JSON sorts keys lexically, normalizes amount strings to scale-4, and strips memo whitespace.
4. SELECT FROM `transfer_idempotency` WHERE `key_hash=?` AND `owner_customer_id=?`.
   - Row exists + checksum equal → return `cached_response_body` with HTTP `cached_response_code`; set `idempotencyStatus=IDEMPOTENT_REPLAY`.
   - Row exists + checksum differ → return 409 IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_CONFLICT (do NOT execute business logic).
   - Row does not exist → INSERT placeholder row `(key_hash, customer_id, status=PENDING, ...)` in same DB transaction as the transfer write; finalize after saga step finishes by UPDATEing the row to COMPLETED / FAILED / REJECTED with the actual response.

### Consequences
- Positive: Raw key never on disk → operator cannot replay against another customer. Per-customer scope prevents cross-customer key collisions. Canonical JSON checksum is deterministic across clients.
- Negative: Canonical-JSON serializer must be agreed across services — must live in `common-libs/idempotency-lib` to prevent drift.
- Trade-off: SHA-256 over HMAC — HMAC was rejected because it adds key-rotation complexity for a value (`raw_key`) that is already a client-generated UUID with no business meaning. Operator cannot rainbow-table a UUID v4 (122 bits of entropy).

## Links
- Solution-Architect ADR-002 (idempotency storage shape)
- OpenAPI `components.schemas.ProblemDetail.code` enum
- `common-libs/idempotency-lib` (shared canonical-JSON serializer)

adrs.title: Outbox relay poll cadence and dispatch semantics
adrs.content:
# ADR-014. Outbox relay poll cadence and dispatch semantics

- Status: accepted
- Date: 2026-05-18
- Deciders: banking-tech-lead
- Tags: messaging, outbox, reliability

## Context and Problem
The Architect's ADR-003 chose the transactional outbox pattern. The Tech Lead must pin the relay's cadence, batch size, and concurrency semantics so backend dev does not invent ad-hoc values that drift between services.

## Decision Drivers
- Customer-perceived latency budget: TransferCompleted must be on Kafka within ~1s of commit so notification-service hits the 60s SLA with comfortable margin.
- Outbox row growth: at 100 TPS sustained, ~360k rows/hour. Need to clear them fast and not pile up.
- Horizontal scaling: multiple service pods must safely dispatch in parallel without duplicates.

## Considered Options
1. Debezium CDC (rejected upstream — see Architect ADR-003).
2. Scheduled poll every 500ms with batch=100, FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED, dispatched=true upon Kafka ack.
3. PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY trigger on outbox INSERT, push-driven.

## Decision Outcome
Chosen: Option 2.
- Cadence: every 500ms (Spring `@Scheduled(fixedDelay = 500)` on a dedicated single-thread executor per pod).
- Batch size: 100 rows per poll, ordered by `created_at ASC`.
- Locking: `SELECT ... WHERE dispatched=false ORDER BY created_at LIMIT 100 FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` so N pods can dispatch in parallel without contention.
- Dispatch: send to Kafka with `acks=all`; on success UPDATE `dispatched=true, dispatched_at=now()`; on failure increment `attempt_count`, store `last_error`, leave dispatched=false for retry.
- Backoff: retries are implicit via the next poll cycle; no in-loop sleep. Hard ceiling: after `attempt_count >= 10` the row is logged at ERROR and an ops alert raised (PagerDuty); we do NOT auto-skip — manual intervention.
- Cleanup: hourly job purges `dispatched=true AND dispatched_at < now() - interval '7 days'`.
- Consumer dedupe: consumers MUST dedupe on `event_id` (the outbox row's UUID) because dispatch is at-least-once.

### Consequences
- Positive: simple, no new infrastructure (Debezium / Kafka Connect avoided). SKIP LOCKED makes horizontal scaling free.
- Positive: 500ms cadence keeps customer-perceived event latency low (notification-service end-to-end remains < 60s comfortably).
- Negative: A 500ms quiet period on TransferCompleted exists even on a single pod. Acceptable per SLA budget.
- Negative: LISTEN/NOTIFY would be lower-latency but adds long-lived DB connections per pod and a fallback path when notifications are dropped — over-engineering for the SLA we need.

## Links
- Solution-Architect ADR-003 (transactional outbox decision)
- `transfer_outbox` table definition (V004)
- Topic naming convention: `<service>.<entity>.<event>.v<n>` per project-structure.md

adrs.title: STRIDE threat model for POST /api/v1/transfers + GET /api/v1/transfers/{id}
adrs.content:
# ADR-015. STRIDE threat model for POST /api/v1/transfers + GET /api/v1/transfers/{id}

- Status: accepted
- Date: 2026-05-18
- Deciders: banking-tech-lead
- Tags: security, threat-model, api-contract

## Context and Problem
New money-moving endpoints must pass a STRIDE walk-through before backend dev starts. This ADR captures threats considered and the mitigation already designed into the contract, so security-agent review later can verify rather than re-discover.

## STRIDE Walk-through

### Spoofing
- Threat: caller impersonates another customer to debit their account.
- Mitigation: bearerAuth (RS256 JWT, 15-min TTL) validated at gateway + service. `sourceAccountId` MUST be cross-checked against the JWT subject's ownership at the service (account-service exposes `accountsByCustomer`). Reject 403 on mismatch. JWT signature verified via JWKS with key rotation (architect NFR security_oauth2_jwt_rs256).

### Tampering
- Threat: attacker replays or modifies an in-flight HTTPS request.
- Mitigation: TLS 1.2+ end to end (architect NFR security_tls_1_2_plus). Idempotency-Key + payload checksum (ADR-013) detect any altered replay (returns 409). Server never trusts client-supplied `transferId`, `referenceNumber`, `completedAt`, or `correlationId` for committed state.

### Repudiation
- Threat: customer denies initiating a transfer.
- Mitigation: Immutable audit-service record per BoT (architect ADR-012). `initiator_user_id`, `correlation_id`, `ip_address` (hashed), `user_agent`, `channel`, `limit_snapshot_*` all persisted on the transfers row + audit_log. Outbox guarantees the audit event survives.

### Information Disclosure
- Threat: error responses leak balance, account holder name, or internal stack traces.
- Mitigation: Problem-Detail `detail` field is SAFE-for-display only (no balance values, no holder names). 500 responses NEVER include exception messages — code returns generic INTERNAL_ERROR; the actual trace is in OTel only, correlated by `traceId`. Account numbers masked to last 4 digits in any log line (observability-lib filter). PDPA: ip_address SHA-256-hashed before persistence.

### Denial of Service
- Threat: high-volume Idempotency-Key flooding fills the table; abusive client floods POST /transfers.
- Mitigation: Gateway rate-limit 60 req/min per customer (architect NFR). transfer_idempotency hourly TTL purge (RISK-004). Resilience4j bulkhead on transfer-service controller (max 200 concurrent). Account-service circuit breaker prevents cascade.

### Elevation of Privilege
- Threat: caller calls POST /transfers with a different customer's source account or escalates scope.
- Mitigation: JWT scopes enforced (`transfers:write` required for POST; `transfers:read` for GET). Service double-checks ownership of `sourceAccountId` via account-service. `compensation_transfer_id` and `saga_id` are server-assigned only — never accepted from the request body.

## Decision Outcome
Threat model passed. No new mitigations required beyond what is already in the contract + architect ADRs. Document referenced from security-agent's checklist when reviewing the implementation PR.

### Consequences
- Positive: backend dev has explicit confirmation of which validations to wire (JWT subject vs sourceAccountId ownership check; scope assertion).
- Positive: security-agent review can use this ADR as a checklist rather than re-derive.
- Negative: requires backend dev to add an `accountsByCustomer` lookup on the hot path — pre-warmed via Redis 60s TTL cache to keep p95 budget.

## Links
- Solution-Architect NFR map (security_*, compliance_pdpa, compliance_bot_circular)
- Solution-Architect ADR-011 (concurrency control)
- Solution-Architect ADR-012 (audit immutability)

adrs.title: Money serialization on the wire: decimal string, not JSON number
adrs.content:
# ADR-016. Money serialization on the wire: decimal string, not JSON number

- Status: accepted
- Date: 2026-05-18
- Deciders: banking-tech-lead
- Tags: api-contract, money, precision

## Context and Problem
JSON numbers are IEEE 754 doubles in most clients (JavaScript especially). Sending `1500.0000` as a JSON number loses scale information and can introduce binary-float artifacts on large amounts. The DB stores `NUMERIC(19,4)` and Avro events use logical-decimal — the HTTP boundary must match.

## Decision Drivers
- Frontend is Angular (TypeScript / JavaScript) — JSON numbers are doubles.
- Customer-visible amount must always render with the bank's chosen scale (2 dp for THB display, 4 dp for internal precision).
- DB and Avro already use exact decimal.

## Considered Options
1. JSON number (e.g. `1500`).
2. JSON string with regex-validated decimal (e.g. `"1500.0000"`).
3. Object with explicit unscaled value + scale (e.g. `{"unscaled": 15000000, "scale": 4}`).

## Decision Outcome
Chosen: Option 2.
- Wire format: `amount` is a JSON string matching `^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]{1,4})?$`.
- Server parsing: `new BigDecimal(amount).setScale(4, RoundingMode.UNNECESSARY)` — throws if client sent more than 4 dp.
- Server serialization: always `setScale(4)` so response is consistent.
- Frontend MUST treat the string as opaque and use a decimal library (e.g., `decimal.js`) for any arithmetic.

### Consequences
- Positive: zero precision loss across JS clients.
- Positive: matches Avro `decimal` logical type + DB `NUMERIC(19,4)`.
- Negative: client developers must remember not to coerce to Number(). Mitigation: frontend codegen wraps amount in a `Money` value type from the generated client.

## Links
- OpenAPI `TransferRequest.amount` schema
- DB `transfers.amount NUMERIC(19,4)`
- Avro logical decimal in TransferRequested.avsc

implementation_notes: Idempotency: wrap the POST /transfers handler in a use-case method annotated @Transactional. The first DB action MUST be the SELECT on transfer_idempotency by (key_hash, owner_customer_id). On hit-with-equal-checksum return cached body. On hit-with-different-checksum return 409 IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_CONFLICT WITHOUT executing business logic. On miss, INSERT a placeholder row with result_status=PENDING in the same transaction as the transfers INSERT; UPDATE it to COMPLETED/FAILED/REJECTED with the final cached_response_body at end of the use case. Use the shared common-libs/idempotency-lib for canonical-JSON serialization to keep the request_checksum algorithm consistent across services (see ADR-013).

Outbox publishing: NEVER call kafkaTemplate.send() directly from the use-case. Instead, INSERT a row into transfer_outbox in the same @Transactional method as the business write. A separate @Scheduled(fixedDelay = 500) bean on a dedicated single-thread executor polls with SELECT ... WHERE dispatched=false ORDER BY created_at LIMIT 100 FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED and dispatches with acks=all. Mark dispatched=true + dispatched_at=now() on Kafka ack. Consumer-side dedupe is on event_id (see ADR-014). The polling pattern must NOT block the user-facing thread pool.

Saga coordination: implement TransferSagaCoordinator as a stateful @Component that drives the state machine STARTED -> LIMIT_CHECK_DONE -> DEBITED -> CREDITED -> COMPLETED. Each transition persists saga_state.current_step + saga_state.version (optimistic lock) BEFORE invoking the next downstream call. On pod restart, a recovery worker scans saga_state WHERE status IN ('IN_PROGRESS','COMPENSATING') AND updated_at < now() - interval '30 seconds' and resumes by replaying the current_step. Saga steps MUST be idempotent — account-service exposes idempotent debit/credit by (transfer_id, step_name) per architect's design.

Optimistic locking on daily_transfer_accumulator (for future US-005 work, but design the entity now with @Version): on OptimisticLockException, retry the entire SELECT + UPDATE once with a fresh read. If still conflicting, return 503 DEPENDENCY_UNAVAILABLE with Retry-After: 1 so the client retries (the same Idempotency-Key keeps the retry safe). Do NOT chain more than one retry inside the request — let the client + Idempotency-Key handle further attempts. Use INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (account_id, accumulation_date) DO UPDATE for the first transfer of the Bangkok day to avoid the midnight race in RISK-002.

Resilience4j on account-service calls: configure a time-limiter (timeoutDuration=800ms), circuit breaker (slidingWindowSize=20, failureRateThreshold=50, waitDurationInOpenState=10s, permittedNumberOfCallsInHalfOpenState=5), and bulkhead (maxConcurrentCalls=50, maxWaitDuration=100ms). Annotate each client method with @TimeLimiter, @CircuitBreaker, @Bulkhead. On TimeoutException or CallNotPermittedException convert to a custom DependencyUnavailableException and let the controller advice map it to 503 DEPENDENCY_UNAVAILABLE with Retry-After: 2.

Transactional boundaries: place @Transactional only on the @Service use-case methods (TransferUseCase.execute), NEVER on @RestController. Reason: a transaction must NOT span the JSON deserialization or HTTP write; the controller is also where exception-to-Problem-Detail mapping happens, which must run outside the transaction so a rollback does not mask the response body. Default propagation=REQUIRED, isolation=READ_COMMITTED (PostgreSQL default; we rely on optimistic locks for concurrency).

Reference number generation: `TRF-YYYYMMDD-XXXXXXXX` where the suffix is uppercase Crockford-base32 of a per-day monotonic sequence pulled from a PostgreSQL sequence `reference_number_seq` (CREATE SEQUENCE in a follow-up migration). MUST be generated server-side after the transfers row PK is committed; the client never sends or chooses it. Uniqueness enforced by the `uq_transfers_reference_number` UNIQUE constraint — on the (~zero-probability) collision, regenerate and retry once.

Logging + masking: every log line uses the observability-lib JSON layout with correlation_id, trace_id, transfer_id, and source_account_id masked to last 4 digits. Use Logback's MaskingConverter (provided by observability-lib) for account numbers and amounts. NEVER call log.info(amount) directly — always log.info("transfer amount: {}", maskedAmount). The amount value is FULLY persisted in the encrypted audit_log table, just not in flat logs (architect NFR compliance_pdpa).

JWT subject ownership check: before any precheck, call account-service GET /api/v1/accounts/by-customer/{customerId} (cached 60s in Redis with customer-scoped key) to assert that sourceAccountId is in the JWT subject's account list. Return 403 FORBIDDEN if not. This guards against the spoofing threat in ADR-015 and is the prerequisite to all balance / limit calls.
frontend_notes: Generate Idempotency-Key as a UUID v4 the moment the user opens the transfer form (NOT on submit-button click). Cache it in component state until the request returns a terminal response (200/400/401/403/409/422). On 503 + Retry-After, automatically retry the same request with the SAME Idempotency-Key after the indicated delay. Only generate a new key after a terminal response or when the user navigates away from the form.

Treat the amount field as an opaque string end-to-end. Use decimal.js (or BigInt with a fixed scale) for any client-side arithmetic (e.g. balance projection display). Never `Number(amount)` — see ADR-016. The generated TypeScript client (from this OpenAPI) will type amount as `string`; do not narrow it.

On HTTP 409 IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_CONFLICT, do NOT retry — show the user an explanatory dialog ("This transfer was already attempted with a different amount; please review and confirm") and force them to re-open the form (which generates a new key).

On HTTP 503 with Retry-After, render a transient banner like "Confirming your transfer..." and auto-retry up to 3 times (with delays from Retry-After, defaulting to 2s if header absent). On 4th failure show "Service is unavailable — your money has not been moved; please try again later." Always include the X-Request-Id from the response in the support contact link so ops can correlate.

Use the response `idempotencyStatus` field to decide messaging: FIRST_WRITE -> show success toast; IDEMPOTENT_REPLAY -> silently update UI state without a duplicate toast (the user clicked submit twice or the network retried).

After any 5xx or socket timeout where the response is unknown, the client SHOULD poll GET /api/v1/transfers/{transferId} every 1s for up to 10s to learn whether the original request committed. The transferId is unavailable until first server response — so the strategy is: cache the Idempotency-Key, retry POST with same key (returns cached response, which contains transferId), then poll if still unknown.

metadata.notes: Tech Lead design handoff for Money Transfer v1 (intra-bank, THB). Scope: backend dev to implement US-001 happy path + US-003 idempotency in transfer-service. OpenAPI covers POST /api/v1/transfers + GET /api/v1/transfers/{transferId} fully; lookupPayee + listTransfers stubbed with x-status: planned. DB schema spans all 11 stories' data needs in 5 reversible Flyway migrations (transfers, transfer_idempotency, saga_state, transfer_outbox, daily_transfer_accumulator). 3 Avro event schemas published (TransferRequested, TransferCompleted, TransferFailed). 4 ADRs filed (ADR-013 error code taxonomy + idempotency hashing, ADR-014 outbox poll cadence, ADR-015 STRIDE threat model, ADR-016 money string-on-wire). 9 implementation notes + 6 frontend notes. Other services' schemas (account-service, payee-service, audit-service, etc.) deferred to per-service tech-lead sessions. No deviations from architect ADRs — all ADRs build on top of architect decisions. Quality gate: contract + schema + threat model all satisfied.
